violations.description: Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee making ice-cream cones and sundaes observed handling ready-to-eat cones and fruit with bare hands.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Cloths used to clean food contact surfaces were left out on the counter.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Shelving in the drive-thru area is chipped and now showing bare particle board, surface is no longer a non-absorbent or cleanable. Bare piece of wood on the walk-in cooler wall is unsealed and is not a cleanable surface.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. The drain line on the compressor in the walk-in freezer is leaking and causing excessive ice build-up.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. The floors in the walk-in freezer, dish area, and in the kitchen need a thorough cleaning.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Wet mop stored on the floor by the front counter was not placed in a proper position to dry.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
